title: Completionist
description: A Data Pack for obtaining all blocks and Items within Minecraft but within a world border.
body:
# Completionist
Completionist is a datapack where the goal is to collect all items within Minecraft. The catch is that you are confined by a world border. This world border can be expanded for every item you obtain.

## Difficulties
There are five difficulties:
1. Easy: World Border expansion has a 3x multiplier
2. Normal: World Border expansion has a 2x multiplier
3. Hard: World Border expansion has a 1x multiplier
4. Challenge: World Border expansion has a 1x multiplier and items are consumed when first obtained.
5. Expert: World Border expansion has a 1x multiplier, items are consumed when first obtained, and there is no rarity multiplier.


## Rarity Multiplier
The rate of expansion is determined by the rarity of the item. Common items expand the border by 1 block radially, Uncommon items expand the border by 5 blocks radially, Rare items expand the border by 12 blocks radially and Epic items expand the border by 25 blocks radially. These rarities are determined by [Minecraft's default rarities](https://minecraft.wiki/w/Rarity).
An exception to the rule is Enchanted Books, expansion from these are determined by a custom determined rarity and enchantment level.

<details>
<summary>open</summary>
  
![Enchanted Book Rarities](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/38d3aa41264394be93de353a7f7aa3d40e8969df.png)
</details>

## Configuration
This datapack has a pre-load config to change aspects of the game before you start!
This config allows you to change aspects of the pack before world creation such as:
1. Difficulty
2. Rarity Multipliers
3. Item Consumption
4. Hotbar Status Titles
5. Structure and Biome Trackers
6. Alternate Game Modes

## Alternative Game Modes
Completionist has three alternative game modes to play with! These gamemodes are not exclusive and can be enabled together if you wanted.

**Borderless**: Play without the border!

**Icarus**: You have the ability to glide and have infinite rockets.

**Arcade Mode**: You start the world with a five minute timer earning an extra minute for each new item you obtain (subject to rarity).

**Inverted**: The world border contracts for every item you obtain until you run out of items to get or you reach the 1x1 end.

## Addons
**Trimmed**: Obtain all trimmed armor pieces (excluding dyed leather)

**Unobtainable**: Blocks that cannot be obtained in the survival inventory reward when stood atop.

**Next Update**: Includes items from the next Update / Drop (pending datapack compatibility)

## Other
Items you have obtained can be monitored through the Advancements screen (I recommend a mod that increases the size of the advancements screen). Many seeds will be soft-locked at the start
